originalTitle: US stocks edge down with Iran and earnings season in focus
summary: STORY: Stocks started the week on a down note, with the Dow falling about six tenths of one percent, while the S&P 500 dropped two tenths and the Nasdaq ended basically flat. :: Archive Investors looked for moves toward de-escalation in the Middle East while they waited for earnings reports due from major technology companies later in the week. Melissa Brown, managing director of investment decision research at SimCorp, says the technology firms will have to post significant earnings growth to maintain their lofty trading levels. “Every company, whether it's Google or Tesla, obviously they have very different business models and different drivers of their business. But overall, I think to justify the high valuations that we see in a lot of those names, we would need to see good earnings growth, not just kind of your run-of-the-mill 10%, but you'd want to see higher earnings growth on top of good margins as well. So, we want the businesses to be profitable and growing.” :: Archive Meanwhile, Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday that they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, opening a new front in the U.S.-Iran war and widening the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. But a senior Iranian official told Reuters that mediators have passed Iran a proposal to de-escalate the war with the U.S. that would offer a 10-day ceasefire to find ways to revive an interim deal reached last month. Stocks on the move included Paramount Skydance which lost two percent and Warner Brothers Discovery which slipped almost four percent after a judge halted their $110 billion merger temporarily as a coalition of states argued it would irreparably harm competition. And shares of Domino’s Pizza gained two percent after the chain’s quarterly revenue edged past Wall Street estimates.

But overall, I think to justify the high valuations that we see in a lot of those names, we would need to see good earnings growth, not just kind of your run-of-the-mill 10%, but you'd want to see higher earnings growth on top of good margins as well. So, we want the businesses to be profitable and growing.” :: Archive Meanwhile, Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday that they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, opening a new front in the U.S.-Iran war and widening the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. chineseTitle: 美国股市小幅下跌，伊朗和财报季成为焦点
chineseSummary: 故事：股市本周开局下跌，道指下跌约十分之六百分之一，标准普尔 500 指数下跌十分之二，纳斯达克指数收盘基本持平。 :: Archive 投资者一边等待本周晚些时候主要科技公司发布的盈利报告，一边期待中东局势缓和的举措。 SimCorp 投资决策研究董事总经理梅丽莎·布朗 (Melissa Brown) 表示，科技公司必须实现显着的盈利增长才能维持较高的交易水平。 “每家公司，无论是谷歌还是特斯拉，显然都有非常不同的商业模式和不同的业务驱动因素。但总的来说，我认为为了证明我们在许多公司中看到的高估值是合理的，我们需要看到良好的盈利增长，而不仅仅是普通的 10%，但你还希望在良好的利润率之上看到更高的盈利增长。因此，我们希望这些业务能够盈利并不断增长。”与此同时，也门与伊朗结盟的胡塞武装周一表示，他们将对沙特阿拉伯实施海上封锁，在美伊战争中开辟一条新战线，并扩大对海湾地区以外全球能源供应和贸易的威胁。但一名伊朗高级官员告诉路透社，调解员已通过伊朗一项缓和与美国战争的提案，该提案将提供为期 10 天的停火，以找到恢复上个月达成的临时协议的方法。受此影响的股票包括派拉蒙 Skydance 下跌 2%，华纳兄弟探索公司 (Warner Brothers Discovery) 下跌近 4%，此前法官暂时中止了这两家公司价值 1,100 亿美元的合并，因为各州联盟认为这将不可挽回地损害竞争。在该连锁店的季度收入小幅上涨后，达美乐披萨 (Domino’s Pizza) 的股价上涨了 2%。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
